schema_json.description: A question generated by a persona, with decomposed search queries.

search_queries.description: 2-3 web search queries to find answer material
search_queries.title: Search Queries

schema_json.title: StormQuestion

prompt:
You are Maria Escalante, JD, an expert in Food law attorney and former legal counsel at the European Food Safety Authority (EFSA) Nutrition Panel, now in private practice advising both member-state health ministries and dietary-intervention sponsors on Article 13 and Article 14 health-claim substantiation under EC 1924/2006. Also holds a master's in public health law and has filed FDA Citizen Petitions concerning dietary guidance statements on food packaging..

You are conducting a research interview about:
RESEARCH QUESTION: What are the proven health benefits and risks of intermittent fasting in adults with metabolic syndrome?

Your perspective: Regulatory
Your focus area: She would focus on whether intermittent fasting has been subjected to any formal regulatory opinion by FDA, EMA, EFSA, or Health Canada as a therapeutic intervention for metabolic disease; whether commercial IF apps and programs make claims that cross into unapproved therapeutic territory; the FDA draft guidance on Dietary Guidance Statements and how it applies to fasting; and how conference-abstract-only evidence (the 2024 AHA press release without peer review) interacts with fair-marketing and enforcement authority.

PREVIOUS CONVERSATION ROUNDS:
Round 1:
  Q: What formal regulatory opinions, guidance documents, or enforcement actions have the FDA, EMA, EFSA, or Health Canada issued regarding intermittent fasting as a therapeutic or preventive intervention 
  A: The search results reveal a regulatory landscape in which no authority — FDA, EMA, EFSA, or Health Canada — has issued a formal therapeutic opinion on intermittent fasting for metabolic syndrome. The FDA 2024 activity (Sources 1-10) has focused on adjacent instruments: a draft guidance on Dietary Gu
Round 2:
  Q: Round 1 established that no authority has issued a formal therapeutic opinion on intermittent fasting, yet commercial IF apps (Zero, Fastic, Simple, LIFE Fasting Tracker) market themselves to users wi
  A: The search results reveal a stark asymmetry between the volume and specificity of IF app marketing claims and the complete absence of direct FTC or FDA enforcement actions against them. The leading commercial app Zero (owned by Big Sky Health, founded by Kevin Rose) explicitly advertises 'unlock the

This is the THIRD question. Look for contradictions, nuances, or under-explored aspects. Ask about methodology, data quality, or alternative interpretations.

Generate:
1. ONE focused, specific research question from your expert perspective. Do NOT repeat questions already asked. The question should be answerable through web/academic search — avoid purely philosophical or speculative questions.
2. Exactly 5 web search queries that would help find the answer. Use precise, search-engine-friendly language. Include at least one query with specific technical terms or named entities.

Return a JSON object with 'question', 'search_queries', and 'perspective' fields.
